text.title: Puppeteer Session Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Each job re-logs in; MFA prompts arrive every run; budget burns on auth, not on actual work.
antipattern.root-cause: userDataDir generated per run (mkdtemp); benefits of persistence lost.
antipattern.fix: Allocate one userDataDir per logical identity; reuse across runs. Snapshot the directory after first successful login.

antipattern.symptom: Parallel jobs see each other's data; user-42 ends up viewing user-13's dashboard.
antipattern.root-cause: All jobs share browser.defaultBrowserContext(); cookies leak across.
antipattern.fix: Wrap each job in browser.createIncognitoBrowserContext() OR start a fresh browser per job. Always close the context in finally.

antipattern.symptom: Per-task invocation costs 4s of Chromium cold-start; pipeline budget eaten by overhead.
antipattern.root-cause: Bash worker launches and tears down Chromium for every task.
antipattern.fix: Move to a daemonised runner exposing an HTTP/queue interface; the agent enqueues tasks and the browser stays warm.

antipattern.symptom: Audit finds session cookies committed in git history.
antipattern.root-cause: userDataDir path was relative to repo root; everything inside got staged.
antipattern.fix: Move userDataDir under /var/lib/agent/profiles/<id>/ or equivalent encrypted-at-rest path; add path pattern to .gitignore as defence-in-depth.

antipattern.symptom: Daemon memory grows linearly with job count; OOM after 200 tasks.
antipattern.root-cause: createIncognitoBrowserContext called per job; context.close() never reached due to throw.
antipattern.fix: Wrap context creation in try/finally; always call context.close(). Add a watchdog logging open context count per cycle.
